title: Add M5 benchmarks, invariants and M6 stop signs
summary: Add M5 long-run benchmarks and invariant gates, record M6 readiness stop-sign evidence, and preserve 10 percent warning / 20 percent blocking thresholds.
acceptance: M5 invariant gate runs the alpha scenario long enough to detect content validation drift anomaly leaks faction/governance hidden authority event queue growth save/replay divergence Worker mismatch and M0-M4 regression

Benchmarks report anomaly roster counts activation candidates transition counts faction/governance candidates event pool candidates cooldowns validation failures save/load rebuild time Worker projection bytes final hashes and M6 stop-sign verdict
Benchmark artifacts include Node pnpm OS CPU commit seed scenario id content manifest hash tick horizon checkpoint hashes final summary and reviewed SHA-256
Baseline updates are reviewed artifacts and preserve 10 percent warning and 20 percent blocking thresholds
M6 readiness is reported as stop signs only; no M6 task is created promoted claimed implemented or reviewed

history.details: Created by WM-0072 M5 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.
Implemented M5 invariant gate, long-run benchmark baseline/artifact, docs and stop-sign closeout; required checks pass; no M6 task created.
verified: Verified M5 invariant gate, benchmark artifact SHA/canonical hash evidence, environment fields, unchanged 10/20 thresholds, M6 stop-sign-only scope, allowed paths, and required gates.

objective: Create artifact-backed M5 performance, invariant and M6 stop-sign evidence before any M6 planning can begin.
forbiddenPaths: product behavior fixes to satisfy tests
silent retries repeatEach or retries changes
schema changes outside approved M5 validation surfaces
M6 task creation promotion claim implementation or review
platform packaging crash reporting external playtest or storefront work
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: benchmark task fixes product behavior; product fixes must be blocked or split.
Risk: M6 readiness report becomes M6 planning; it may record stop signs only.
Risk: artifact hash evidence is ambiguous; report must name exact file SHA-256 and any canonical payload digest separately.
benchmarkImpact: Adds reviewed M5 long-run benchmark artifacts and baseline changes only with explicit 10/20 percent threshold preservation and M0-M4 regression evidence.
reviewRouting: Qa-performance completes to reviewer; project-director integrates only after artifact evidence and required checks are reproducible and no M6 task exists.
rollbackModel: Revert M5 benchmark tests artifacts baseline changes stop-sign notes and report; leave M5 open and do not advance M6.
ownerGate: Name exact artifact paths and hashes in the report
Do not fix product behavior inside the benchmark task
Do not create promote claim implement or review M6 work
Block on unreproducible artifacts threshold weakening protocol save schema dependency UI authority or M6 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Benchmark baselines, long-run invariant gates and milestone stop signs require qa-performance ownership and independent review.

review.summary: Verified M5 invariant gate, benchmark artifact SHA/canonical hash evidence, environment fields, unchanged 10/20 thresholds, M6 stop-sign-only scope, allowed paths, and required gates.
